asymmetry: NHS dementia patients are being sedated against their will and physically prevented from getting out of bed — not by rogue staff, but as routine practice uncovered in the first-ever national review.
hook: They're sedating dementia patients to keep them quiet.
core_fact: An 18-month national review — the first of its kind — found dementia patients routinely chemically sedated and physically restrained in their beds across NHS care settings in the UK.
villain: NHS care institutions enabling systematic sedation and restraint of dementia patients as standard operational practice.
share_trigger: Did you see this? They're sedating dementia patients just to make them easier to manage — and it's been happening everywhere.
search_query: NHS dementia patients sedated restrained care homes UK report 2024 2025
